name: aws
context: Terraform AWS provider — configure, authenticate, and manage AWS resources with HCL including EC2, S3, VPC, IAM, Lambda, and RDS
info: The AWS provider for Terraform is the most widely used provider by download count, enabling declarative management of hundreds of AWS service resources. It translates HCL resource blocks into AWS API calls, handling create, read, update, and delete operations across EC2, S3, VPC, IAM, Lambda, RDS, ECS, and many more services. Authentication follows the standard AWS credential chain: environment variables, shared credentials file (~/.aws/credentials), IAM instance profiles, and OIDC federation. The provider supports assume_role for cross-account access, default_tags for consistent tagging, and aliases for multi-region deployments. Data sources let you query existing AWS resources (AMIs, VPCs, caller identity) without managing them.
triggers: use assume role in terraform aws
set default tags in terraform aws

sections.title: Provider configuration
sections.body: Declare the AWS provider in `required_providers` with `source = "hashicorp/aws"` and a version constraint (e.g., `~> 5.0`). The `provider "aws"` block accepts `region` (required in most setups), `profile` (named AWS CLI profile), `default_tags` (consistent tagging), and `allowed_account_ids` (safety check preventing deployment to wrong accounts). The provider reads configuration from environment variables (`AWS_REGION`, `AWS_DEFAULT_REGION`) when arguments are omitted.

sections.title: Authentication and credential chain
sections.body: The AWS provider resolves credentials in order: (1) static credentials in the provider block (not recommended), (2) environment variables `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID` and `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` plus optional `AWS_SESSION_TOKEN`, (3) shared credentials file `~/.aws/credentials` selected by `AWS_PROFILE` or the `profile` argument, (4) EC2 instance metadata / ECS task role, (5) OIDC web identity via `AWS_WEB_IDENTITY_TOKEN_FILE`. For cross-account access, use `assume_role` with a `role_arn`, optional `session_name`, and optional `external_id`. For CI/CD, prefer OIDC federation (GitHub Actions, GitLab) over long-lived access keys.

sections.title: Most-used AWS resources
sections.body: The most commonly managed AWS resources in Terraform: `aws_instance` (EC2 virtual machines), `aws_s3_bucket` and `aws_s3_bucket_versioning` (object storage), `aws_vpc`, `aws_subnet`, `aws_security_group` (networking), `aws_iam_role` and `aws_iam_policy` (access control), `aws_lambda_function` (serverless compute), `aws_db_instance` (RDS databases), `aws_ecs_service` and `aws_ecs_task_definition` (container orchestration), `aws_route53_record` (DNS), and `aws_cloudwatch_log_group` (logging). Each resource type maps directly to an AWS API resource and supports full lifecycle management.

sections.title: Most-used AWS data sources
sections.body: Data sources read existing AWS resources without managing them. `data.aws_ami` looks up AMIs by owner and filters (essential for EC2). `data.aws_vpc` and `data.aws_subnet` query existing network infrastructure. `data.aws_caller_identity` returns the current account ID, user ARN, and user ID. `data.aws_region` returns the current region. `data.aws_availability_zones` lists available AZs for the current region. `data.aws_iam_policy_document` generates JSON IAM policy documents from HCL, which is more readable than inline JSON.

sections.title: Multi-region and multi-account deployments
sections.body: Use the `alias` meta-argument to configure multiple instances of the AWS provider targeting different regions or accounts. Resources reference aliased providers with `provider = aws.<alias>`. For multi-account, combine aliases with `assume_role` blocks pointing to different account role ARNs. Pass aliased providers to child modules using the `providers` map in the `module` block: `providers = { aws = aws.eu }`. This pattern enables deploying identical infrastructure across regions for disaster recovery or compliance.

scripts.name: Verify AWS Credentials
scripts.description: Verify AWS credentials are configured correctly, display the caller identity, current region, and list available resource types in the Terraform AWS provider
scripts.inline:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

echo '=== AWS Credential Verification ==='
echo ''

# Check if AWS CLI is available
if ! command -v aws &>/dev/null; then
  echo 'ERROR: aws CLI not found. Install it from https://aws.amazon.com/cli/'
  exit 1
fi

# Verify credentials by calling STS
echo '--- Caller Identity ---'
if IDENTITY=$(aws sts get-caller-identity --output json 2>&1); then
  echo "$IDENTITY" | python3 -c "
import sys, json
d = json.load(sys.stdin)
print(f\"  Account:  {d['Account']}\")
print(f\"  UserId:   {d['UserId']}\")
print(f\"  ARN:      {d['Arn']}\")
" 2>/dev/null || echo "$IDENTITY"
else
  echo "ERROR: AWS credentials not configured or invalid."
  echo "  Set AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID and AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, or run 'aws configure'."
  echo "  Error: $IDENTITY"
  exit 1
fi

echo ''
echo '--- Current Region ---'
REGION=${AWS_REGION:-${AWS_DEFAULT_REGION:-$(aws configure get region 2>/dev/null || echo 'not set')}}
echo "  Region: $REGION"

echo ''
echo '--- Available Services (sample) ---'
for SVC in ec2 s3 iam lambda rds ecs sts; do
  if aws $SVC help &>/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "  [OK] $SVC"
  else
    echo "  [--] $SVC (not available or not authorized)"
  fi
done

echo ''
echo '--- Terraform Provider Check ---'
if command -v terraform &>/dev/null; then
  echo "  Terraform version: $(terraform version -json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c 'import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)["terraform_version"])' 2>/dev/null || terraform version | head -1)"
else
  echo '  Terraform not installed. Install from https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/install'
fi

echo ''
echo 'Credentials verified successfully.'

AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID.description: IAM access key identifier (optional if using shared credentials or instance profile)

AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY.description: IAM secret access key (optional if using shared credentials or instance profile)

AWS_SESSION_TOKEN.description: Temporary session token for assumed roles (optional)

AWS_PROFILE.description: Named AWS CLI profile to use from ~/.aws/credentials

region.description: AWS region for the provider (e.g., us-east-1)

assumeRoleArn.description: ARN of the IAM role to assume for cross-account access

defaultTags.description: Default tags applied to all resources

accountId.description: AWS account ID from caller identity

depends.reason: The AWS provider is a Terraform provider plugin
IAM credentials and roles are required for provider authentication
